behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the file scripts/create-github-release.mjs, the exact import format, execFileSync from node:child_process, and 'git' / 'show' literals used in tests.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction essentially dictates the fix: exact import string 'import { execSync, execFileSync } from \'node:child_process\'', exact first arg 'git', and array form starting with 'show' — this mirrors the gold patch precisely.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests accept any execFileSync('git', ['show', ...]) call and the exact import format; alternative correct fixes matching these constraints pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All core f2p tests (test_execfilesync_imported, test_git_show_uses_execfilesync, test_no_shell_injection_in_rel_path, test_execfilesync_has_array_args) are regex checks on file contents; only syntax/lint/prettier subprocesses run, and none actually execute the script to verify injection is prevented.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each requirement in instruction.md (import format, execFileSync usage, no relPath interpolation, syntactic validity) maps to a test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings labeling f2p vs p2p.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task modifies a script, no structured data output.
